meta.document: DFR Intelligence Source Registry — US + Global
meta.description: Comprehensive registry of all intelligence feeds for the DFR vertical. US primary + global secondary. Each source tagged with region, cadence, data category, and miner status.

sources.name: DRONERESPONDERS (AIRT)
sources.description: World's leading nonprofit for public safety UAS. Blog, monthly webinars, national conference (March annually, Williamsburg VA), UNITE training program, geo-referenced global DFR program directory (with ESRI + NASA Ames). Director: Chief Charles Werner.
sources.notes: Also hosts DRONERESPONDERS Public Safety Summit at Commercial UAV Expo (Las Vegas, May 2026) — $375 pass. Critical GTM venue.

sources.name: Public Safety Drone Review (DRONERESPONDERS + DroneLife)
sources.description: Free monthly webcast co-hosted by DRONERESPONDERS and DroneLife. Features FAA officials, agency program managers, vendor presentations. March 2026 covered counter-UAS and FIFA World Cup 2026 security planning.

sources.name: AIRT — Airborne International Response Team
sources.description: Parent organization of DRONERESPONDERS. 501(c)3. Manages training events, mission-critical deployments globally, XPONENTIAL Europe DRONERESPONDERS program.

sources.name: DroneLife
sources.description: Editor-in-Chief Miriam McNabb. Highest-volume DFR trade coverage in the US. Covers BRINC, Skydio, Flock, regulatory changes, FCC updates daily. RSS available.
sources.notes: RSS: https://dronelife.com/feed — high signal-to-noise ratio for DFR

sources.name: Police1
sources.description: Primary law enforcement trade publication. DFR section with program case studies, vendor announcements, tactical analysis. Agency testimonials sourced here.

sources.name: Police Magazine
sources.description: Secondary LE publication with DFR and drone technology coverage.

sources.name: Commercial UAV News
sources.description: Public safety drone section. Vendor-agnostic coverage of DFR programs and technology trends.

sources.name: DroneDJ
sources.description: Strong product-focused DFR coverage. Good for vendor announcements and product launches.

sources.name: UAV Coach
sources.description: Comprehensive DFR program guide updated for 2026. Community forum has agency operator discussions.

sources.name: Autonomy Global
sources.description: Deeper policy and strategy analysis on public safety UAS. 2026 UAS transitions article is reference-quality.

sources.name: UAS Magazine
sources.description: BBI International. Part 108 / 14 CFR 108 coverage, BVLOS, UAS workforce certification. Less DFR-specific but good regulatory depth.

sources.name: Unmanned Systems Technology (UST)
sources.description: Media sponsor of XPONENTIAL 2026 Public Safety Pavilion. Global coverage. AirData Public Safety Program announcement sourced here.

sources.name: FAA BEYOND / Advanced Operations
sources.description: Primary FAA BVLOS waiver and authorization source.

sources.name: Federal Register — FAA Rulemakings
sources.description: Part 108 and all FAA NPRM/Final Rule publications. API available.

sources.name: DHS Authorized Equipment List (AEL)
sources.description: AEL item 03OE-07-SUAS — primary DFR grant eligibility reference.

sources.name: OJP — Drone as First Responder Practical Insights
sources.description: Office of Justice Programs published report defining DFR programs, key technologies, operational/governance considerations. Reference-quality authoritative source.

sources.name: Grants.gov
sources.description: Federal grant opportunity database. API available for keyword monitoring.

sources.name: COPS Office
sources.description: DOJ COPS Technology grant program. Direct application source for LE agencies.

sources.name: NUSTL — National Urban Security Technology Laboratory
sources.description: DHS S&T lab. Published DFR TechNote (25_0708_st_dfrtn.pdf). Testing standards for counter-UAS at World Cup 2026. Key policy source.

sources.name: EFF Atlas of Surveillance
sources.description: Electronic Frontier Foundation maps all known DFR programs by city. Privacy/civil liberties perspective — valuable for understanding governance backlash and program distribution.

sources.name: MuckRock
sources.description: Public records platform. DFR flight logs, use policies, FOIA-responsive documents from agencies. EFF directs users here for DFR transparency. Valuable for governance intelligence.

sources.name: Skydio Newsroom / Press Releases
sources.description: DFR Command now at 10M+ calls for service (Feb 2026). 25+ integrated public safety systems. NG911 integration dispatches drone before CAD entry. 3,800+ agency deployments. Key source for CAD integration depth updates.
sources.key_intel: DFR Command: 10M calls, 25+ system integrations, CJIS-compliant evidence chain, NG911 pre-CAD dispatch

sources.name: BRINC Newsroom
sources.description: BRINC Guardian launched March 24 2026 — first Starlink-connected drone, most capable 911 response drone. 900+ agency deployments, 25%+ of US SWAT teams. NLC partnership (2,600+ cities). Seattle factory.
sources.key_intel: Guardian: Starlink connectivity, auto-dispatch, most capable 911 drone per BRINC. NLC exclusive partnership.

sources.name: Flock Safety Blog / Newsroom
sources.description: Flock911, Alpha drone, Aerodome acquisition coverage. DFR ecosystem integration with ALPR network.

sources.name: Versaterm / DroneSense
sources.description: MAJOR UPDATE: Versaterm acquired DroneSense (July 2025), then acquired Aloft (Feb 2026 — FAA-approved USS, powers majority of LAANC authorizations in US). DroneSense now integrates directly with Versaterm CAD dispatch — drones dispatched like patrol/fire/EMS units. Critical CAD integration intelligence.
sources.key_intel: Versaterm = DroneSense + Aloft + CAD. Single vendor now controls fleet management + airspace authorization + CAD dispatch for many agencies.

sources.name: AirData Public Safety Program
sources.description: AirData launched Public Safety Program March 2026. 444K+ pilots, 232 countries, 60M flights. Public Portal for transparency (Chula Vista, Las Vegas Metro, Sacramento PD use it). Fusus and Milestone integration. Multi-drone live view.
sources.key_intel: AirData Public Portal = community transparency tool for DFR. Used by Ohio DOT for Ohio DFR Pilot fleet management.

sources.name: DRONERESPONDERS National Public Safety UAS Conference
sources.description: Annual, March, Williamsburg VA. FAA leadership presents. 2026: Paul Strande (FAA Deputy Executive Director) opened event. Key regulatory signal source.

sources.name: Commercial UAV Expo + DRONERESPONDERS Summit
sources.description: Las Vegas, May 2026. DRONERESPONDERS Summit co-located. $375 pass. After-action reviews from LE/fire/EMS/SAR. Key GTM and intelligence venue.

sources.name: AUVSI XPONENTIAL 2026
sources.description: May 11-14 2026, Detroit MI. 8,500+ attendees. Public Safety Pavilion (UST media sponsor). Global UAS industry conference.

sources.name: ALEA Annual Conference
sources.description: Airborne Law Enforcement Association. Key law enforcement aviation/drone community.

sources.name: IACP Annual Conference
sources.description: International Association of Chiefs of Police. IACP Aviation Committee. Key policy and adoption signals.

sources.name: FCC Covered List — Drone Conditional Approvals
sources.description: MAJOR UPDATE March 18 2026: FCC introduced first Conditional Approvals for drone systems — 4 systems approved through Dec 31 2026. New pathway alongside Blue UAS and Green UAS. Critical for grant eligibility matrix updates.
sources.key_intel: FCC Conditional Approval = new compliance pathway. 4 systems approved Mar 17 - Dec 31 2026. Does not replace NDAA/Blue UAS requirements.

sources.name: AUVSI Green UAS Certification
sources.description: Industry-led cybersecurity and supply chain vetting program for non-DoD/commercial operators. Complements Blue UAS for public safety procurement.

sources.name: Route Fifty
sources.description: Municipal government technology publication. Best source for city/county DFR budget decisions, NLC partnership coverage, program expansion news.

sources.name: GovTech
sources.description: State and local government technology. DFR program coverage from the procurement/IT side.

sources.name: Everdrone (Sweden)
sources.description: World leader in drone emergency medical services (DEMS). AED delivery ops since 2020. New E3 drone (24kg, 80km/h, 4.5kg payload, SORA-certified BVLOS). Operations: Västra Götaland, Stockholm (Sweden), Normandy (France via SAMU), UK (KSS Air Ambulance, Project Lifeline). SEK 36M raised March 2026. GridPlanner tool for coverage analysis. Clinical trial with Karolinska Institutet 2026.
sources.key_intel: AED arrives before ambulance in 64% of cases, median 2-3 min time advantage. EU operational permit covers all of EU. Expanding to Nordic + France + UK.
sources.relevance_to_us: Validated clinical model for AED/payload delivery use case. UK £20M government investment shows government funding pathways. Regulatory approach (SORA) relevant to US Part 108 comparison.

sources.name: EENA — European Emergency Number Association
sources.description: European 112 emergency services association. Everdrone partnership for AED drone clinical trials. Policy and standards for emergency drone integration across EU member states.
sources.relevance_to_us: EU regulatory model may inform US Part 108 rulemaking. EENA standards track ahead of US in medical drone integration.

sources.name: UK Department for Transport — AAM Programme
sources.description: UK government announced £20M+ package to accelerate advanced air mobility including DEMS. DfT + DSIT wrote to CAA requesting longer operational authorisations, faster NHS integration. Project Lifeline: KSS Air Ambulance + Everdrone + NATS + Gatwick Airport + Altitude Angel.
sources.key_intel: UK = £20M government investment, NHS integration target, CAA fast-track for emergency drone authorisations. Comparable to US BEYOND waiver overhaul.
sources.relevance_to_us: Funding model and NHS integration pathway as comparative intelligence for US healthcare grant programs.

sources.name: XPONENTIAL Europe / DRONERESPONDERS European Forum
sources.description: DRONERESPONDERS runs European Public Safety and Emergency Response Forum at XPONENTIAL Europe (Düsseldorf 2025; 2026 TBD). AIRT partners with European organizations. Covers firefighting, SAR, event security, disaster response.

sources.name: African Drone Forum
sources.description: Growing global AI/autonomy ecosystem. Pavilion at XPONENTIAL Europe 2026. Medical drone delivery expanding in Africa (Rwanda model, Zipline). Intelligence signal for global DFR market expansion.

sources.name: EASA — European Union Aviation Safety Agency
sources.description: EU drone regulatory authority. U-space framework (European UTM). SORA (Specific Operations Risk Assessment) — the EU equivalent of FAA waiver safety case. Everdrone's BVLOS permit covers all EU under EASA framework.
sources.relevance_to_us: SORA methodology may inform Part 108 safety case requirements. U-space = EU UTM framework comparable to US FAA UTM development.

sources.name: UK CAA — Civil Aviation Authority
sources.description: UK drone regulator post-Brexit. Separate from EASA. DfT pressing CAA for faster emergency drone authorisations. Project Lifeline authorization pathway.

sources.name: Karolinska Institutet — Drone Emergency Research
sources.description: Leading clinical research institution for medical drone efficacy. 2026 clinical trial with Everdrone and VGR. Published in Lancet and NEJM. OHCA drone data: AED before ambulance in 64% of cases, 2-3 min median advantage.
sources.relevance_to_us: Clinical evidence base for AED delivery use case — directly applicable to US DFR payload delivery reports.

sources.name: Journal of the American Heart Association
sources.description: 23-year study: early AED intervention by first responders → 46.2% survival rate. Core evidence for AED delivery DFR use case. Referenced by Motorola Solutions DFR guide.

sources.name: IDTechEx — Drones Market 2026-2036
sources.description: Premium market research. Public safety expected to remain stable expanding segment through 2036. Military leads revenue but public safety has strong government backing. Risk-based regulatory harmonization globally.
sources.notes: Paid report — use for high-level market sizing in DFR State Program reports.

sources.name: Verizon Frontline Public Safety Communications Survey
sources.description: 2025 survey: daily drone use in public safety projected to triple over next 5 years. Referenced by Motorola Solutions DFR guide.

sources.name: DroneLife — Global Coverage
sources.description: Also covers XPONENTIAL Europe, African Drone Forum, Everdrone, global vendor announcements. Same RSS feed as US coverage — filter by region tag.

intel_updates_from_research.description: New intelligence found during this research session — needs to update existing database records

updates.finding: BRINC Guardian launched March 24 2026 — world's first Starlink-connected drone, 'most capable 911 response drone ever'. Adds to Lemur 2 lineup. 900+ agency deployments, 25%+ of US SWAT teams.
updates.action: Add Guardian to BRINC platform record. Update deployment count.

updates.finding: Versaterm acquired DroneSense (July 2025), then acquired Aloft (Feb 2026). DroneSense now integrates directly with Versaterm CAD — drones dispatched like patrol/EMS units. Aloft = majority of US LAANC authorizations.
updates.action: Update DroneSense entry: now Versaterm DroneSense. Add Aloft as UTM layer. Update CAD integration depth from 'api_integration' to 'native_with_cad'.

updates.finding: Skydio DFR Command: 10M+ calls processed, 25+ public safety system integrations, CJIS-compliant, NG911 pre-CAD dispatch (drone launches before call enters CAD). Also integrates: RapidSOS, Motorola Command Aware, STRAX, Genetec, Milestone, Dedrone Beyond (counter-UAS), ALPR cameras.
updates.action: Expand Skydio CAD integration list. Add CJIS compliance flag. Add NG911 pre-dispatch capability note.

updates.finding: Paladin Knighthawk 2.0 launched — new NDAA-compliant DFR platform. IdeaForge Q6 V3 (Indian manufacturer, 550K flights, public safety focus) available in US market via Drone Nerds.
updates.action: Add Knighthawk 2.0 and Q6 V3 as emerging platforms to track. Flag for next platform database update.

updates.finding: AirData Public Safety Program (March 2026) — 444K pilots, 232 countries, 60M flights. Public Portal for community transparency. Fusus + Milestone integration. Ohio DFR Pilot uses AirData for fleet management.
updates.action: Add AirData as new CAD/fleet management system entry.

updates.finding: FCC Conditional Approval (March 18 2026): 4 drone systems granted temporary approval through Dec 31 2026. New compliance pathway alongside Blue UAS and Green UAS. Does not replace NDAA requirements.
updates.action: Add FCC Conditional Approval as new compliance pathway in BEYOND guide and grant eligibility matrix.

updates.finding: BRINC NLC partnership: BRINC is exclusive drone partner for National League of Cities — 2,600+ member cities. NLC partnership functions as a quasi-distribution channel for BRINC to municipal agencies.
updates.action: Add NLC partnership note to BRINC grant eligibility record. Note that BRINC has preferential access to NLC-member city procurement processes.

updates.finding: FIFA World Cup 2026 (US/Canada/Mexico) = major DFR procurement trigger. DRONERESPONDERS + Commercial Drone Alliance + White House Task Force coordinating Nov 2025. Public safety agencies actively buying for World Cup security. Narrow window — procurement happening now.
updates.action: Add FIFA World Cup 2026 as near-term GTM trigger. Agencies procuring for World Cup = active buyers. Target outreach at FIFA host city agencies (Los Angeles, Dallas, Miami, New York, Seattle, Philadelphia, Kansas City, San Francisco/Bay Area, Houston, Boston).

updates.finding: OJP (Office of Justice Programs) published authoritative DFR implementation report. Fetch and index as reference-quality source.
updates.action: Build web_fetch task for OJP DFR report URL. Index in regulatory data category.

priority_1_build_next: dronelife.com RSS (US + global — highest DFR signal volume)
